note: Copy quality is subjective and graded qualitatively. The objective, load-bearing assertion is A (no fabrication): the skill exists to enliven the user's real facts without inventing new ones. Baselines (no skill) run on the fabrication-critical cases to measure the delta. To re-run: hand each prompt to an agent that has read SKILL.md + references/, and grade against the assertions.

assertions.A_no_fabrication: CRITICAL, objective. No concrete specific (number, feature, customer name, testimonial, price) appears in the output that was not in the input draft/facts. Disclosed synonyms, framing, and mechanism that follow from a given fact are allowed; new claims are not.
assertions.B_asks_when_absent: When facts are missing, the response asks the user for the real specifics instead of producing invented copy.
assertions.C_facts_preserved: When facts are present, every given number/name/price is reproduced exactly, none distorted or overclaimed (e.g. a median is not promised as a guarantee).
assertions.D_goal_structure: Output uses a recognizable structure appropriate to the stated goal (PAS/AIDA=sell, BAB/open-loop=reach, PMHS/stance=provoke, QUEST/grounded=trust).
assertions.E_linter_floor: Response treats the linter as the floor (re-lints, picks the right --target). Soft/advisory.

evals.prompt: Make this landing blurb sell. Goal: sell. Draft: "Our new tool helps engineering teams ship faster. It's powerful and easy to use. Thousands of developers love it. Sign up today." (No additional facts about the product are provided.)
evals.expect: Asks for the missing specifics; invents nothing. Assertions A + B.

evals.prompt: Make this sell. Goal: sell. Draft: "Acme CI runs your tests. It's fast and reliable. Try it." Real facts: hosted CI service; median suite runs in 6 minutes vs ~40 minutes on teams' previous setup; free for open source; public reference customers Linear and Vercel; setup is a single YAML file; paid plans start at $30 per seat per month.
evals.expect: PAS/AIDA copy using only the given facts; numbers/names exact. Assertions A + C + D.

evals.prompt: Rewrite this so people share it. Goal: reach. Draft: "We wrote a post about how we cut our CI time. Read it." Real facts: reduced CI from 40 minutes to 6 by sharding the test suite and caching Docker layers; the post includes the exact config; we're a 5-person team.
evals.expect: BAB or open-loop; the 40->6 after-state as the shareable hook. Assertions A + D.

evals.prompt: Make this spark a discussion. Goal: provoke. Draft: "We think most CI setups are too slow." Real facts: stance is that teams tolerate 40-minute CI because they've never priced the wait; we measured it at ~$2,000/month in idle waiting at our team's salaries; we cut ours to 6 minutes.
evals.expect: PMHS or a clear defensible stance; uses the $2,000 figure that is given, manufactures no extra pain. Assertions A + D.

evals.prompt: Rewrite this intro so it builds trust for a long guide. Goal: trust. Draft: "Our guide explains how to speed up CI. Read on." Real facts: long-form guide covering test sharding, Docker layer caching, dependency caching, and parallelism; we run a hosted CI product but the guide is tool-agnostic; the author has 10 years in DevOps.
evals.expect: QUEST or grounded exposition; qualify audience, educate, soft transition; no invented customers or competitor tool names. Assertions A + D.
